incarceration.definition: The act of confining, or the state of being confined; imprisonment.

incidence.definition: The act of something happening; occurrence.

inclined.definition: To bend or move (something) out of a given plane or direction, often the horizontal or vertical.

inclusion.definition: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.

incur.definition: To bring upon oneself or expose oneself to, especially something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to become liable or subject to

indicator.definition: A pointer or index that indicates something.

indictment.definition: An official formal accusation for a criminal offence, or the process by which it is brought to a jury.

indigenous.definition: Born or originating in, native to a land or region, especially before an intrusion.

induce.definition: To lead by persuasion or influence; incite or prevail upon.

indulge.definition: (often followed by "in"): To yield to a temptation or desire.

infamous.definition: Having a bad reputation, disreputable; notoriously bad, unpleasant or evil; widely known, especially for something bad.

infant.definition: A very young human being, from birth to somewhere between six months and two years of age, needing almost constant care and/or attention.

infect.definition: To bring into contact with a substance that causes illness (a pathogen).

inherent.definition: Naturally as part or consequence of something.

inhibit.definition: To hold in or hold back; to keep in check; restrain.

initiate.definition: A new member of an organization.

inject.definition: To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.

injection.definition: The act of injecting, or something that is injected.

inmate.definition: A person confined to an institution such as a prison (as a convict) or hospital (as a patient).

insertion.definition: The act of inserting, or something inserted.

insider.definition: A person who has special knowledge about the inner workings of a group, organization, or institution.

inspect.definition: To examine critically or carefully; especially, to search out problems or determine condition; to scrutinize.

inspection.definition: The act of examining something, often closely.

inspiration.definition: The drawing of air into the lungs, accomplished in mammals by elevation of the chest walls and flattening of the diaphragm, as part of the act of respiration.

instinct.definition: A natural or inherent impulse or behaviour.

institutional.definition: Of, pertaining to, characteristic of, or organized along the lines of an institution.

insult.definition: Action or form of speech deliberately intended to be rude; a particular act or statement having this effect.

intact.definition: Left complete or whole; not touched, defiled, sullied or otherwise damaged

intake.definition: The place where water, air or other fluid is taken into a pipe or conduit; opposed to outlet.

integral.definition: A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.

integrated.definition: To form into one whole; to make entire; to complete; to renew; to restore; to perfect.

integration.definition: The act or process of making whole or entire.

integrity.definition: Steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code.

intensive.definition: Form of a word with a stronger or more forceful sense than the root on which the intensive is built.

intent.definition: A purpose; something that is intended.

interactive.definition: A feature (as in a museum) that can be interacted with.

interface.definition: The point of interconnection or contact between entities.

interfere.definition: To get involved or involve oneself, causing disturbance.

interference.definition: The act of interfering with something, or something that interferes.

interim.definition: A transitional or temporary period between other events.

interior.definition: The inside of a building, container, cavern, or other enclosed structure.

intersection.definition: The junction of two (or more) paths, streets, highways, or other thoroughfares.

intervene.definition: To become involved in a situation, so as to alter or prevent an action.

intervention.definition: The action of intervening; interfering in some course of events.

intriguing.definition: To conceive or carry out a secret plan intended to harm; to form a plot or scheme.

inventory.definition: (operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.

invisible.definition: An invisible person or thing; specifically, God, the Supreme Being.

invoke.definition: To call upon (a person, a god) for help, assistance or guidance.

involvement.definition: The act of involving, or the state of being involved.

ironic.definition: Characterized by or constituting (any kind of) irony.

ironically.definition: (manner) In an ironic manner; in a way displaying irony.

irony.definition: A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.

irrelevant.definition: Not related, not applicable, unimportant, not connected.

isolation.definition: The state of being isolated, detached, or separated.

J.definition: A term for a marijuana cigarette ('joint').

judicial.definition: That branch of government which is responsible for maintaining the courts of law and for the administration of justice.

jurisdiction.definition: The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.

justification.definition: A reason, explanation, or excuse which provides convincing, morally acceptable support for behavior or for a belief or occurrence.

K.definition: A kilobyte (more formally KB or kB).

kidnap.definition: The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.

kidney.definition: An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.

kingdom.definition: A realm having a king and/or queen as its actual or nominal sovereign.

landlord.definition: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.

landmark.definition: An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).

lap.definition: The loose part of a coat; the lower part of a garment that plays loosely; a skirt; an apron.

laser.definition: A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.

latter.definition: Relating to or being the second of two items.

lawmaker.definition: One who makes or enacts laws.

lawsuit.definition: In civil law, a case where two or more people disagree and one or more of the parties take the case to a court for resolution.

layout.definition: A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.

leak.definition: A crack, crevice, fissure, or hole which admits water or other fluid, or lets it escape.

leap.definition: The act of leaping or jumping.

legacy.definition: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.

legendary.definition: A collection of legends, in particular of lives of saints.

legislation.definition: The act of legislating; preparation and enactment of laws; the laws enacted.

legislative.definition: That branch of government which is responsible for making, or having the power to make, a law or laws.

legislature.definition: A governmental body with the power to make, amend and repeal laws.

legitimate.definition: A person born to a legally married couple.

lengthy.definition: Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.

lesbian.definition: A homosexual woman, one who is mostly or exclusively sexually or romantically attracted to other women.

lesser.definition: A thing that is of smaller size, value, importance etc.

liable.definition: Bound or obliged in law or equity; responsible; answerable.
